summary: PR 2234 made focused matching fixes in ftCo_Attack100.c around CaptureDamageLw functions. The diff replaces a broad dont_inline pragma block with static inline helper bodies plus exported wrapper functions, cleans up generated temp_r30-style locals in one path, preserves a narrow PAD_STACK(4) in fn_800DC624, and the bot report records three new matches in the ftCo_Attack100 unit.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updates CaptureDamageLw Phys/Coll-related matching code and fn_800DC624.

what_changed.text: Removed the local #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop block that previously surrounded ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_IASA, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and fn_800DC624.
Converted ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys into a wrapper that calls static inline ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline.

Reworked ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys internals from temp_r30/temp_r31 temporaries to direct gobj usage plus fp/fp2 locals, while keeping calls to fn_800DAD18, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, fn_800DB5D8, fn_800DAA40, ft_80083C00, and HSD_JObjSetTranslate.
Converted fn_800DC624 into a wrapper that uses PAD_STACK(4) and calls static inline fn_800DC624_inline.
Simplified fn_800DC624_inline by using gobj directly and passing &temp_r31->cur_pos directly to HSD_JObjSetTranslate instead of storing a separate Vec3* local.
The decomp bot report recorded 3 new matches: fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.

smart_moves.text: The PR narrowed codegen steering by removing a broad dont_inline pragma region and using explicit inline helper/wrapper structure around only the affected functions.
The source cleanup replaced unclear generated-looking temp_r30 usage in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys with direct gobj usage and role-style Fighter* locals.
The remaining PAD_STACK tactic was scoped to fn_800DC624's wrapper, and the bot report provides narrow evidence that fn_800DC624 reached 100%.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a dont_inline pragma block is only being used to shape a small cluster, consider replacing it with static inline helper bodies plus thin exported wrappers so codegen steering is scoped to the functions that need it.
Generated temp_rXX locals can sometimes be cleaned into direct parameter usage and clear role locals without losing the match; in this PR ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys still reached 100%.
If PAD_STACK is required, keep it narrow and pair it with match evidence for the exact symbol rather than hiding it inside a broad pragma region.

naming_conventions.text: The diff moves one function away from temp_r30/temp_r31 locals toward direct gobj usage and short Fighter* locals named fp/fp2, consistent with conservative role naming when local roles are clear.
The static helper names use the existing function name plus an _inline suffix: ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline and fn_800DC624_inline.
The address-style symbol fn_800DC624 was not semantically renamed; the PR only introduced fn_800DC624_inline for its helper.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace a broad dont_inline pragma block with static inline helpers plus non-inline wrappers.
Use PAD_STACK(4) only in fn_800DC624's wrapper before calling fn_800DC624_inline.
Adjust local lifetime/access shape by using direct gobj calls, separate Fighter* fp/fp2 reads, and direct &temp_r31->cur_pos passing where appropriate.
Use bot-reported per-symbol match results as the narrow verification ledger for the touched functions.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code 55.46% (+0.02%, +672 bytes) and 3 new matches: fn_800DC624 (+200 bytes, 33.64% to 100.00%), ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys (+185 bytes, 41.59% to 100.00%), and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll (+20 bytes, 65.86% to 100.00%).

follow_up_queries: GET_FIGHTER fp fp2 local lifetime matching
remove dont_inline pragma static inline helper melee decomp

notes: The changed file list and full diff are available and untruncated.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The bot report gives concrete match results, but no compiler command, local objdiff output, or author explanation is included.
Intent behind the inline-wrapper shape and PAD_STACK use is inferred from the diff plus match report, not explicitly stated by the author or reviewer.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Scope inline/codegen tactics with helper wrappers instead of broad pragmas
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2234 shows a focused matching fix where a broad #pragma dont_inline region was removed and replaced with static inline helper bodies plus thin wrappers for ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys and fn_800DC624. The bot report recorded those symbols as new 100% matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep PAD_STACK narrow and tied to symbol-level match evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2234 keeps PAD_STACK(4) only in the fn_800DC624 wrapper before calling fn_800DC624_inline, and the bot report records fn_800DC624 moving to 100%. This supports the existing principle that stack-shaping tactics need narrow verification evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting inline-helper wrappers as a narrow alternative to broad pragma regions
source_update_candidates.text: Possible standards addition: when removing a broad dont_inline pragma would otherwise disturb matching, try static inline helper bodies plus thin exported wrappers before keeping a broad pragma scope. PR 2234 is supporting evidence, but the slice lacks author explanation and full objdiff logs.
source_update_candidates.reason: The diff demonstrates this pattern with bot-reported new matches, but it should be curator-reviewed before becoming a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that PAD_STACK(4) is generally required for fn_800DC624-like functions; this slice only supports its use in this specific wrapper with this bot report.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human reviews or review comments are present.
Do not promote ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll source details beyond the bot-reported match because the diff excerpt only shows nearby context, not a substantive Coll body edit.
